description: Manual decisions for residual wrapper candidate plans and their promotion status.

decisions.reason: This candidate wraps the already-active posttooluse-bash-pre-guard-async and posttooluse-bash-post-guard-async wrappers around the direct dangerous-command-detect hook. A direct promotion would hide the intentional split-sync boundary.
decisions.next_action: Keep the current split-sync Bash layout until a dedicated Bash router can preserve sync dangerous-command detection and async post-processing separately.

decisions.reason: This is a narrower side-effect view of the same PostToolUse/Bash router problem. It overlaps the active posttooluse-bash-post-guard-async wrapper and the direct dangerous-command-detect hook.
decisions.next_action: Resolve together with posttooluse-bash-event-matcher; do not migrate this side-effect subset independently.

decisions.reason: SessionEnd/*는 shared matcher를 쓰는 저블럭 경로로, health-notify + badge-clear를 route 방식으로 묶을 적합도가 높아 1개 후보로 먼저 선별한다.
decisions.next_action: sessionend-all-event-matcher를 planned wrapper로 등록하고 activation gate + dry-run/execute 증빙을 확보한 뒤 settings migration으로 진행한다.

pretooluse_guard_policy.description: Direct-vs-wrapper decisions for current PreToolUse guards. This section covers active wrappers, planned wrappers, and guards that must remain direct.

decisions.reason: These generic Bash safety gates already share a blocking execution contract and are active as one wrapper. The wrapper preserves first nonzero exit and stops after the first blocking child.
decisions.next_action: Keep active. Only expand this wrapper with additional Bash guards if they share the same fail-closed blocking semantics and do not require a separate status/output policy.

decisions.reason: Commit hooks are contiguous and share the same trigger, but they mix LLM-backed advisory steps with hard blocking checks. They remain planned until isolated execute and statusMessage policy are reviewed for settings migration.
decisions.next_action: Keep planned. Promote only as one unit after activation gate review; do not keep individual commit guards direct once the wrapper is active.

decisions.reason: PR hooks share a trigger and preserve stdout/status feedback together. This is the smallest planned PreToolUse wrapper by child count and only runs for gh pr commands, so it has the lowest settings migration blast radius among planned candidates.
decisions.next_action: Keep active. If gh pr preflight behavior changes, compare wrapper execution against the two original child hooks before expanding this wrapper.

decisions.reason: Edit/Write guards share one mutation boundary. The delegation enforcer can block, while the other hooks are advisory or side-effecting, so one wrapper must preserve first blocking exit and stdout/stderr passthrough. Wrapper has been moved to active settings for safer single-point policy and consistent step ordering.
decisions.next_action: Monitor for regression in edit/write flows and compare wrapper-first nonzero exit behavior against the previous direct child order.

decisions.reason: Agent hooks form one pre-agent context/notification/prescan bundle. They are now promoted together to keep context stdout, audio side effects, and review prescan cache writes in one ordered path.
decisions.next_action: Monitor for agent launch regressions and validate that first-failing child behavior remains visible under wrapper execution.

decisions.reason: This guard protects deferred MCP tool calls before schemas are loaded. It is a narrow protocol safety gate and should remain direct until a dedicated MCP router exists.
decisions.next_action: Keep direct. Revisit only with a separate MCP router design and fixture coverage for blocked deferred calls.

decisions.reason: This guard mitigates the known AskUserQuestion non-ASCII serialization bug. It should stay direct to minimize latency and avoid masking the hard block.
decisions.next_action: Keep direct until the upstream serialization bug is fixed and verified against Korean payload fixtures.
